title: Vectors, Matrices, Transformations, and Data Systems · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Represent flows and transformations across a small service system. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a matrix-based flow and transformation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use matrix dimensions to validate addition, multiplication, and transformations.
Interpret matrices as transformations of vectors and coordinate systems.
Solve invertible systems and distinguish existence from numerical stability.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Calculate the unfamiliar quantity
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (3 × 6) − (1 × 2).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (3 × 6) − (1 × 2).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0 of the exact value.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Matrix Applications applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build transition, network, and resource matrices from domain relationships.
